title: ardisia crenata f. xanthocarpa
intro: Ardisia crenata f. xanthocarpa, also known as the golden-fruited ardisia, is a variety of the ardisia crenata plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which may help to treat various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ardisia crenata f. xanthocarpa include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
